description: Trigger discrimination for SEQUENCING/communicating work over time under uncertainty (this skill) vs RANKING the backlog (prioritization-frame-picker), vs one feature's SPEC (product-spec-writer), vs one feature's ROLLOUT (feature-flag-rollout-strategist).

cases.prompt: Build a roadmap that survives changing priorities: horizons, confidence that fades with distance, and sequencing that tests our riskiest assumptions first.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary) — horizons, confidence decay, and learning-first sequencing over time are roadmap planning. No single ranking method and no one-feature spec is being produced.
cases.reason: Sequencing and communicating an uncertain plan over time is this skill's core scope; it consumes a ranking rather than producing one.

cases.prompt: We can't agree how to rank our backlog. Pick the right prioritization framework and score the items with it.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (within pack) — choosing and applying a framework to rank items is prioritization. The roadmap consumes that ranking; it doesn't create it.
cases.reason: prioritization-frame-picker produces the ranked WHAT. This skill sequences the ranked set over time.

cases.prompt: For the top roadmap item, write the detailed product spec: problem, scope, scenarios, and acceptance criteria.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (within pack) — detailing one feature's requirements is a spec, a level below the roadmap.
cases.reason: product-spec-writer specifies one feature. The roadmap places and sequences features; it does not detail their requirements.

cases.prompt: Now plan how we actually ship that top item to users behind a flag — staged percentages, guardrails, and a kill switch.
cases.reason: feature-flag-rollout-strategist plans one feature's flag-gated delivery. The roadmap sequences which features happen when, not how one is rolled out.
